prompts.text: Walk us through your typical Saturday morning, start to finish.
Tell us about a meal you cook for yourself when no one is watching.
Describe the route you take when you go for a walk to clear your head.
Walk us through how you decide what's worth spending money on.
Tell us the story of how you met your closest friend.
Describe how you pack for a weekend trip.
Talk through your process for making a hard decision.
Tell us about a place from your childhood and what it was like to be there.
Walk a friend through how to apologize when they actually messed up.
Explain how you decide which invitations to say yes to and which to decline.
Describe the last time you changed your mind about something important.
Walk us through what a great day off looks like for you.
Tell the story of a time you got lost and how you found your way back.
Explain how you'd teach a kid to ride a bike.
Walk us through how you'd plan a surprise party for someone you love.
Describe your honest relationship with sleep over the last few years.
Tell us about the slowest year of your life and what it taught you.
Walk us through the moment you realized a relationship in your life had quietly changed.
Talk through how your idea of success has shifted from age 18 to now.
Describe, in order, the small habits that have actually made your life better.
Tell us three things you've changed your mind about in the last year.
Share three small luxuries you refuse to give up.
Name the three best gifts you've ever received and why each one mattered.
Walk us through the three rules you'd give your younger self about friendship.
Tell us three moments from your life you'd want to relive exactly as they were.
Share three things you used to believe about adulthood that turned out to be wrong.
Explain why most people get bad at giving feedback as they get older.
Make the case for three habits everyone should try before turning thirty.
Tell us about three people who shaped how you handle conflict.
Lay out three reasons long friendships are harder to maintain than people admit.
Walk a younger sibling through the three biggest money mistakes to avoid.
Explain three signs a relationship is in trouble before anyone says it out loud.
Share three things you do to make a stranger feel welcome.
Tell us about three meals that mark turning points in your life.
Argue for three small acts of courage everyone should practice.
Walk us through three quiet regrets people carry but rarely talk about.
Make the case for three traditions worth keeping even when they're inconvenient.
Explain three ways your home reflects who you actually are.
Lay out three reasons forgiveness is harder than the books make it sound.
Tell us about three silences in your life that changed something.
Tell us how you feel about your own handwriting.
Describe your favorite chair and why you keep coming back to it.
Share what your morning looks like before anyone else is awake.
Tell us about a smell that takes you back somewhere immediately.
Describe the last thing that made you laugh hard.
Tell us about a sound that means home to you.
Explain why you keep a particular object you'd never throw away.
Talk about the friend you call when something good happens, and why it's them.
Describe a compliment you got that you still think about.
Tell us about a conversation you wish you could have again.
Explain how you handle the moment right after someone insults you.
Talk about the last time you cried and whether you understand why.
Describe what loneliness actually feels like for you, not in general.
Tell us about a stranger who made your day better.
Talk about something you used to love and lost the taste for.
Describe what you do when you can't sleep at three in the morning.
Tell us about a fear you've been carrying that you've never named out loud.
Talk about a person who hurt you, and what part of that you still own.
Describe the version of yourself you most want to grow into.
Tell us what you'd say to the people who loved you when you were a child if they were here right now.
Tell us the single best piece of advice you've ever been given.
Share the rule you live by that other people would find strange.
Tell us your favorite word and what it does for you.
Name the one thing you wish more people understood about you.
Tell us the one trait you most respect in other people.
Share the thing you're prouder of than you let on.
Make three claims about what makes a good friend, and stand behind each one.
Argue for three habits that are worth more than people give them credit for.
Tell us three things you believe about love that you'd defend to anyone.
State the case for the city or town you live in, in three claims.
Make three statements about what kids actually need from the adults around them.
Land three reasons honesty is sometimes the worst choice.
Argue three things that matter more than money but only after you have some.
Make three claims about how to be a good guest in someone else's home.
State three things you've stopped apologizing for.
Land three claims about why people stay in situations they shouldn't.
Argue three reasons most weddings get the speeches wrong.
Make three claims about what it actually means to be a grown-up.
State three beliefs about loyalty that you'd defend in a room of skeptics.
Land three things you'd put on a plaque at the entrance to your own life.
Tell us about the most generous thing someone has ever done for you.
Describe a place that always calms you down, in detail.
Tell us about the last time you felt genuinely proud of yourself.
Describe your favorite hour of the day and what it usually contains.
Tell us about a teacher, coach, or mentor who saw you before you saw yourself.
Describe the simplest meal that always feels like a treat to you.
Walk us slowly through the most memorable trip you've ever taken.
Tell us about the first time you understood what your parents had given up for you.
Describe the moment you realized you were finally an adult.
Tell us about a conversation that quietly changed the direction of your life.
Describe what unconditional love has looked like, in your actual experience.
Walk us through the last time you forgave someone and meant it.
Tell us about a small kindness you witnessed that stuck with you.
Describe the longest hug you remember, and the context around it.
Tell us about something you used to be afraid of and how that fear softened.
Walk us through a goodbye that you didn't realize was the last one.
Tell us about the home you would build if money were no object.
Describe the last time you let yourself be fully seen by someone.
Walk us slowly through what you hope someone says about you when you're gone.
Tell us about the kind of love you're still learning how to receive.
Explain what you do on a typical evening after dinner.
Describe the friends you spend the most time with and what you do together.
Tell us how you usually get ready for bed.
Walk us through how you choose what to read or watch next.
Describe your favorite store to walk around in, even when you don't buy anything.
Tell us about how you celebrate your own birthday.
Explain how you handle a friend who's going through a rough patch.
Walk us through how you'd plan the perfect weekend with the people you love.
Describe how you'd help a new neighbor feel at home.
Tell us how you decide when a friendship is worth fighting for.
Explain how you bounce back after a really bad day.
Walk us through how you'd introduce yourself to a room of strangers you actually want to impress.
Describe how you keep track of the people who matter to you.
Tell us how you handle disagreement at a family dinner.
Explain how you decide when to speak up and when to let something slide.
Walk us through what you do in the first hour of a vacation.
Tell us how your friendships have changed over the last ten years.
Explain how you've learned to ask for help, and how long that took.
Walk us through how you'd say goodbye to a place you've lived a long time.
Describe what it looks like when you're at your absolute best around other people.
Tell us about a word you love saying out loud.
Describe your favorite season and what it does to your mood.
Tell us about a meal that's worth every calorie.
Share the name of a place that has weight for you and explain why.
Tell us the song you'd want playing as you walked into a room of friends.
Describe the one quality you'd most want in a future partner or close friend.
Make the case for the single most important relationship in your life.
Tell us about the one trait your closest friend has that you envy in a good way.
Describe the most beautiful place you've ever stood, and the word you'd use for it.
Tell us the one belief that has steered most of your big decisions.
Share the compliment you've never forgotten and the word in it that landed.
Argue for the one virtue you think is most undervalued today.
Tell us about a single moment you'd label the happiest of your life so far.
Describe the one habit you want to be remembered for.
Share the one promise you'd never break, and the word that anchors it.
Tell us the single most important thing you've learned about people.
Name the one thing you'd protect from change in your life and tell us why.
Argue for the one word that, if you lived by it, would change everything.
Tell us the one thing you'd want a child of yours to truly understand.
Share the single sentence you'd want carved into the door of your home.
Make the case that boredom is underrated.
Argue that handwritten letters are still worth sending.
Make the case for going to bed earlier than your friends.
Argue that cooking for yourself is an underrated form of self-respect.
Make the case that walking is the most underrated form of exercise.
Argue that small talk is more important than people pretend.
Make the case that most arguments would end faster if everyone slowed down.
Argue that saying no is one of the most generous things you can learn.
Make the case for keeping in touch with people from your past.
Argue that most people give up on hobbies too early.
Make the case that the best gifts cost very little.
Argue that long phone calls are the most underrated form of love.
Make the case that we should be much more careful with the word friend.
Argue that traveling alone teaches things traveling together never will.
Make the case that you can tell a lot about someone by how they treat waiters.
Argue that nostalgia is more useful than people think.
Make the case that most people would be happier if they owned fewer things.
Argue that the people who shaped you the most didn't know they were doing it.
Make the case that quiet courage is the most important kind.
Argue that the life worth wanting is mostly built out of small, repeatable days.
In 45 seconds: what's the case for living in the city you live in?
In 45 seconds: introduce yourself to someone you'd love to make friends with.
In 45 seconds: pitch your favorite hobby to someone who has never tried it.
In 45 seconds: describe the perfect weekend.
In 45 seconds: tell us your three favorite things about your best friend.
In 45 seconds: recommend a book, show, or movie everyone should try.
In 45 seconds: give a toast at a friend's birthday dinner.
In 45 seconds: convince someone to take a chance on a person they've been hesitating about.
In 45 seconds: explain how you'd plan a great first date.
In 45 seconds: introduce a friend who's joining your group for the first time.
In 45 seconds: pitch your hometown to someone deciding whether to visit.
In 45 seconds: explain what you'd do with a free, fully paid day off tomorrow.
In 45 seconds: argue for one tradition your family should keep forever.
In 45 seconds: make the case for forgiving someone you've been holding a grudge against.
In 45 seconds: introduce yourself the way you wish people would describe you.
In 45 seconds: pitch a friend you love to a stranger as worth knowing.
In 45 seconds: give the eulogy your favorite teacher deserved.
In 45 seconds: deliver the speech you'd give at your own retirement party.
In 45 seconds: make the case for the version of yourself you're trying to grow into.
In 45 seconds: tell the story of your life as a single arc, with a beginning, a turn, and a now.
